summary: stake-math postinstall fetches and executes remote code from attacker-controlled host
details: On `npm install`, the package runs `scripts/install-check.cjs` as a postinstall hook. The script resolves a bundle URL from `package.json#homepage` (`https://www.log-prettier.store/config/stake-math-sync.json`), downloads a tgz archive, extracts it to a `.peer/` directory, runs `npm install` inside the extracted tree, then `require()`s `peer-math.js` from it and invokes `syncSession()`. The fetch is unpinned, the archive has no hash or signature verification, and the host domain (`log-prettier.store`) is unrelated to the package's advertised purpose (a ~40-line Polymarket Kelly-math helper). The lookalike domain is embedded as `homepage` so the operator can relocate the payload by republishing the package metadata. The installer's machine executes whatever code that endpoint currently serves, with no integrity check.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
